title: Minimize the Maximum of Two Arrays
titleCn: 最小化两个数组中的最大值

testcases.input: divisor1 = 2, divisor2 = 7, uniqueCnt1 = 1, uniqueCnt2 = 3 Output: 4 Explanation: We can distribute the first 4 natural numbers into arr1 and arr2. arr1 = [1] and arr2 = [2,3,4]. We can see that both arrays satisfy all the conditions. Since the maximum value is 4, we return it.
testcases.expectedOutput: 4 Explanation: We can distribute the first 4 natural numbers into arr1 and arr2. arr1 = [1] and arr2 = [2,3,4]. We can see that both arrays satisfy all the conditions. Since the maximum value is 4, we return it.

testcases.input: divisor1 = 3, divisor2 = 5, uniqueCnt1 = 2, uniqueCnt2 = 1 Output: 3 Explanation: Here arr1 = [1,2], and arr2 = [3] satisfy all conditions. Since the maximum value is 3, we return it.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: Here arr1 = [1,2], and arr2 = [3] satisfy all conditions. Since the maximum value is 3, we return it.

testcases.input: divisor1 = 2, divisor2 = 4, uniqueCnt1 = 8, uniqueCnt2 = 2 Output: 15 Explanation: Here, the final possible arrays can be arr1 = [1,3,5,7,9,11,13,15], and arr2 = [2,6]. It can be shown that it is not possible to obtain a lower maximum satisfying all conditions.
testcases.expectedOutput: 15 Explanation: Here, the final possible arrays can be arr1 = [1,3,5,7,9,11,13,15], and arr2 = [2,6]. It can be shown that it is not possible to obtain a lower maximum satisfying all conditions.
